question.text: For the purpose of voluntary delisting, a "small company" must have paid-up capital not exceeding _______ AND net worth not exceeding _______.

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Under SEBI Delisting Regulations, a small company for delisting purposes must have paid-up capital not exceeding Rs. 10 crore AND net worth not exceeding Rs. 25 crore. Both conditions must be satisfied simultaneously. **The Trap:** "Rs. 25 crore; Rs. 50 crore" doubles the actual thresholds and may appear logical for candidates who recall only one of the two numbers. Remember: 10 crore (capital) and 25 crore (net worth). **Cross-Exam Context:** The Companies Act, 2013 has its own "small company" definition with different thresholds — do not confuse them. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Small company delisting has a simplified process with tighter timelines (75 working days) — verify both thresholds at the outset to determine eligibility.
